entry.title: Re: Integrating Joule with SAP SuccessFactor
entry.content:
Really helpful overview of Joule integration with SAP SuccessFactors. The blog explains how Joule becomes a context-aware HR copilot once properly integrated, supporting tasks like generating job descriptions, summarizing performance reviews, browsing help content, and automating common HR actions.

Key setup points from the blog and related SAP resources:

1. Prerequisites

  • Ensure SAP SuccessFactors is integrated with SAP IAS (Identity Authentication Service) with SSO.

  • Users must be replicated into IAS and have a unique Global ID that matches between SuccessFactors and IAS.

  • The Joule subaccount region must match the region where SAP Start is available (e.g., both in EU20). Otherwise you’ll hit region errors.

2. Core integration steps

The step-by-step guide outlines:

  1. Establish trust between SAP BTP and IAS

    • This allows Joule to authenticate users through IAS.

  2. Configure user attributes in IAS

    • Joule needs basic user details (first name, last name, email) for personalization.

  3. Launch Joule Booster in SAP BTP

    • Use the automated Joule Booster to simplify configuration.

  4. Add trusted domains in IAS for SuccessFactors

    • This enables secure communication between SuccessFactors and Joule.

  5. Assign Joule access via Permission Roles in SuccessFactors

    • In Admin Center → Manage Permission Roles → choose roles that need Joule → activate Joule Access.

    • Only users with the appropriate permission roles can use Joule.

  6. Test and confirm integration

    • Validate that Joule appears in SuccessFactors and can perform tasks like:

      • Providing feedback (Performance & Goals)

      • Requesting information (Employee Central)

      • Changing personal info

      • Promoting employees

      • Creating job descriptions and interview questions

      • Scheduling 1:1 meetingscrescenseinc+1

3. Extending Joule with custom skills

  • Custom enhancements can be built using Joule Studio and SAP Build.

  • You can create custom skills and expose custom APIs via Integration Suite / SAP BTP (called Actions in SAP Build).

  • These custom skills are then deployed to the global Joule instance and become available to all users.

4. Monitoring and usage

  • Joule Analytics can be configured to monitor usage and messages.

  • Once you reach the included message limit, additional messages are charged accordingly, so planning is important.

This integration is a strong example of how Joule is deeply embedded in SAP cloud apps, using real-time HR data to provide context-aware, conversational support rather than generic AI answers.sap+1

Great guide for both functional and technical teams planning Joule in SuccessFactors.

entry.title: Re: SAP Business Data Cloud and Datasphere News in April
entry.content:
Great April roundup of SAP Business Data Cloud and SAP Datasphere updates. The post highlights how the April release focuses on practical, enterprise-ready improvements in monitoring, task chains, data products, governance, and performance—rather than just big announcements.

Key highlights from the April 2026 release:

1. Monitoring & Cockpit enhancements

  • BDC Cockpit monitoring improvements give better visibility into data product health, system status, and operational metrics.

  • This helps teams spot issues faster and manage Business Data Cloud operations more proactively.

2. Task chain & orchestration improvements

  • Task chains now support “delete all” and “delete filtered records” directly inside orchestration workflows, simplifying data cleanup and refresh scenarios.

  • You can define batches on transformation flows to avoid out-of-memory errors when using the SAP HANA runtime, and choose whether batching applies to initial load, delta load, or both.

  • These changes make large-data orchestration more reliable and easier to tune.

3. Data product versioning & upgrades

  • One-click minor version upgrades for data products inside a space streamline the rollout of incremental changes.

  • Data product versioning updates improve governance and traceability across releases.

4. Connectivity & data sharing

  • Private connectivity to Google BigQuery via SAP Cloud Connector is now supported, removing the need for public endpoints.

  • This aligns with BDC Connect’s broader strategy of zero-copy sharing and secure connectivity to external data platforms (Databricks, BigQuery, Snowflake, and later Microsoft Fabric).

5. Semantic modeling enhancements

  • Semantic calculation views from SAP HANA Cloud can now be imported into SAP Datasphere while preserving metadata, improving reuse and reducing modeling effort.

  • This strengthens the shared semantic layer that BDC is built around.

6. Performance & scalability

  • Local table partitioning can now be added to existing tables without requiring full data reloads, improving performance for large datasets.

  • Apache Spark compute configuration enhancements give more control over resource usage and cost.

The April 2026 update is a strong example of SAP’s 2026 focus: moving from roadmap and architecture to real-world, production-ready capabilities in Business Data Cloud. It’s clear that BDC is positioning itself as the data and analytics backbone for SAP’s AI, analytics, and automation ecosystem.simplefisolutions+1

Nice to see such a practical, feature-rich release ahead of SAPPHIRE.

entry.title: Re: Building a Cost Center Hierarchy Analytical Model in SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Very practical. The post walks through building a cost-center hierarchy analytical model in SAP Datasphere in a way that’s directly reusable for real Finance and Controlling use cases (ICO, EPIC, plan vs. actual, etc.).

Key points that stand out:

What problem it solves

  • Finance teams often need cost-center hierarchies for:

    • Profitability analysis

    • Budgeting and planning

    • Cost allocation and reporting

    • Operational vs. structural views (e.g., cost center group vs. organizational hierarchy)

  • Doing this manually in Excel or spreadsheets is fragile. Building it in Datasphere makes it repeatable, governed, and reusable by SAC and other tools.

How the model is built

The blog shows a typical pattern:

  1. Source data

    • Import or connect to cost center master data and hierarchy data (e.g., from S/4HANA, CO tables such as CSKS, COSP, COST, or planning tables).

    • Use Data Builder to create tables or virtual tables.

  2. Create a semantic model

    • Build a semantic model in Datasphere that defines:

      • Dimensions: Cost Center, Cost Center Group, Hierarchy Node, Period, Version, etc.

      • Measures: Actual costs, Plan costs, Variances, etc.

    • Mark the relevant fields as key figures and attributes.

  3. Build the hierarchy

    • Use hierarchy features in the semantic model or via derived views to:

      • Represent parent-child relationships

      • Define multiple hierarchies (e.g., operational, reporting, planning)

    • Enable hierarchy navigation so SAC can drill up/down the cost center structure.

  4. Create an analytical model

    • Expose the semantic model as an Analytical Model so:

      • SAP Analytics Cloud can consume it directly.

      • You can define planning scenarios (e.g., plan vs. actual) on top.

    • This is the layer that SAC uses for stories, dashboards, and planning.

Why this approach is strong

  • Reusability: The same analytical model can feed multiple SAC stories and planning scenarios.

  • Governance: Hierarchies and cost data are defined in one place, not scattered across multiple Excel files.

  • Performance: Leverages SAP HANA’s analytical capabilities (aggregations, hierarchies, calculated columns).

  • Planning support: You can extend this to planning models for budgeting, forecasting, and allocations.

This is exactly the kind ofFinance-ready reusable asset that makes Datasphere + SAC a strong combo for controlling, FP&A, and cost analysis.simplefisolutions+1

Great reference for anyone building cost-center hierarchies, CO-PA models, or planning scenarios in Datasphere.

entry.title: Re: Time Dependency in SAP Business Partner: Maintenance and Data Distribution to Non-Time-Dependent
entry.content:
Very useful overview of time dependency in SAP Business Partner. The post clearly explains how time dependency works for BP addresses, roles, and relationships, and why it matters for data distribution and integration.

Key points that stand out:

1. What time dependency means for Business Partner

  • By default, time dependency for BP General Data is inactive, so when a BP is created, the system assigns unlimited validity (Valid To = 12/31/9999).

  • When time dependency is activated, you can maintain Valid From and Valid To dates for:

    • BP addresses

    • BP roles

    • BP relationships

  • This allows you to track historical changes and view BP data as it was valid at a specific key date.

  • 2. Address time dependency

  • Time-dependent addresses let you maintain validity periods for multiple addresses per BP (e.g., old vs. new address over time).

  • When time dependency for addresses is active, each address has a validity period.

  • If you change the setting and deactivate time dependency after data has been maintained time-dependently, you can get errors when editing the BP.

  • SAP Note 3225167 describes a common error where time-dependent data is maintained even though time dependency is inactive, often due to Address Independent Communication data (phone, email) having a non-empty Valid From date.

3. Role and relationship time dependency

  • BP roles (e.g., Customer, Vendor, Supplier, Account Holder) can be time-dependent, using Valid From and Valid To fields.

  • BP relationships can also be time-limited by entering start and end dates, so you can track periods when a company was a shareholder, subsidiary, contact person, etc.

  • Time dependency is especially important for:

    • Financial Services (Account Management)

    • M&A scenarios

    • Reporting on historical relationshipssap+1

4. Impact on data distribution

  • When time dependency is active, data distribution must respect validity periods; otherwise downstream systems may get inconsistent or incorrect data.

  • If time dependency is turned on/off incorrectly, you can get terminations in change mode or inconsistent IDOCs.

  • SAP recommends not changing time dependency settings manually or via reports after production data is in the system, unless you fully understand the impact and follow the SAP note procedures.

5. Practical considerations

  • Activate time dependency in Customizing only if you truly need historical tracking and validity periods.

  • If you already have time-dependent data and need to change settings:

    • Process all pending IDOCs first.

    • Follow SAP-provided reports (e.g., in SAP Note 910725) to safely deactivate time dependency.

  • Use key-date reporting to view BP data as valid at a specific point in time.

This is essential background for anyone implementing S/4HANA Customer/Vendor integration, cross-system BP distribution, or historical reporting on business partners.

Great reference for avoiding common time-dependency pitfalls in BP maintenance and distribution.

entry.title: Re: CPI Process Colorizer and Where used global search - Chrome extensions with the help of Vibe cod
entry.content:
Great post! These two Chrome extensions (created with Vibe coding) are real productivity boosters for CPI developers:

  • CPI Process Colorizer – visually colorizes iFlow processes and subprocess call boxes, and adds a searchable "iFlow Processes" panel directly in the BPMN model.

  • Where-Used Global Search – gives you a fast, global "where-used" search across your CPI tenant, so you can quickly find all references to a specific artifact.

Both are lightweight, side-by-side with the well-known CPI Helper extension, and make navigating large CPI landscapes much easier.

entry.title: When creating a sales order , we want to automatically add additional line items
entry.content:
We used Custom Logic and attempted to INSERT into the "SALESORDERREQUESTITEMS" table using "APPEND" at the extension point "SD_SOR_AUTO_XTRCTN_MODIFY".

The error occurred at the statement: "INSERT SALESORDERREQUESTITEMS FROM TABLE lt_items."

We want to keep the extension within Custom Logic as much as possible, without using API-based BTP extensions.

【Question】

１．Is there a correct way to automatically add sales order line items when creating a sales order in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?

⇒ If it can be achieved within Custom Logic, please tell us the available Custom Logic that can be used.

⇒ If it cannot be achieved with Custom Logic, please tell us the correct way to achieve automatic addition.Would it be more appropriate to use BTP Event Mesh or similar solutions? 

 

＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝

The Custom Logic content is as follows【ABAP KeyUser】:
" Manually define structure based on image fields
TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_sales_order_request_item,
salesorderrequest         TYPE c LENGTH 15,   " Sales Order Request
salesorderrequestitem     TYPE n LENGTH 6,    " Sales Order Request Item (numeric)
product                   TYPE c LENGTH 40,   " Product
requestedquantity         TYPE p LENGTH 8 DECIMALS 3,  " Requested Quantity
requestedquantityunit     TYPE c LENGTH 3,    " Requested Quantity Unit
requestdeliverydate       TYPE d,             " Requested Delivery Date
underlyingpurchaseorderitem TYPE c LENGTH 6,  " Underlying Purchase Order Item
materialbycustomer        TYPE c LENGTH 35,   " Material Used by Customer
" Add more as needed...
END OF ty_sales_order_request_item.

DATA: lt_items TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ty_sales_order_request_item,
ls_item  TYPE ty_sales_order_request_item.

" Add first line
ls_item-salesorderrequest     = '000001'.
ls_item-salesorderrequestitem = '000010'.
ls_item-product               = 'MAT001'.
ls_item-requestedquantity     = '10.000'.
APPEND ls_item TO lt_items.

" Add second line
ls_item-salesorderrequestitem = '000020'.
ls_item-product               = 'MAT002'.
ls_item-requestedquantity     = '5.000'.
APPEND ls_item TO lt_items.

INSERT SALESORDERREQUESTITEMS FROM TABLE lt_items.

＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝

entry.title: Using WS_DELIVERY_UPDATE_2 in a Loop
entry.content:
Hi All,

I've stumbled upon a strange behaviour when calling WS_DELIVERY_UPDATE_2 in a loop.
Given 2 valid vbeln, when being executed individually both gave success response and VL33N showed an intended result.
But when using it in a loop, somehow the 2nd document gave error message instead, which consists of HU of the 1st document, even though no HU is provided in the parameter. Any ideas on successfully solving this?

Existing pseudocode looks like:

LOOP AT lt_payload_grouped INTO ls_payload_grouped.
"Some assignment logic here...
  CALL FUNCTION 'WS_DELIVERY_UPDATE_2'
      EXPORTING
        vbkok_wa               = lw_fm_head
        commit                 = 'X'
        synchron               = 'X'
        delivery               = lv_fm_delivery
        if_database_update_1   = '1'
        if_error_messages_send = ''
      TABLES
        vbpok_tab              = lt_fm_item
        prot                   = lt_fm_prott
      EXCEPTIONS
        error_message          = 4
        OTHERS                 = 99.
    READ TABLE lt_fm_prott WITH KEY msgty = 'E' TRANSPORTING NO FIELDS.
    IF sy-subrc = 0.
      CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_TRANSACTION_ROLLBACK'.
    ELSE.
      CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT'
        EXPORTING
          wait = 'X'.
    ENDIF.
ENDLOOP.


Thanks,
Rahman.

entry.title: Re: Issue with provisioning user assignment to Subaccount Administrator role in BTP Subaccount
entry.content:
Hi Guido,

Given your scenario, I have also experienced a similar error message as follows for the same scenario.

 

error=HTTP operation failed invoking https://api.authentication.XXXX.hana.ondemand.com/Groups/Subaccount%20Administrator with statusCode: 400, Response: {"error_description":"Cannot remove all members of admin role collection created by initial content.","error":"scim","message":"Cannot remove all members of admin role collection created by initial content."},

 

Given the error messages we have identified, it would appear that the SCIM process will try to create/update/delete any "role collection created by initial content" on SAP BTP Subaccount (SAP BTP XS Advanced UAA (Cloud Foundry)) target system. I would have expected SAP to include safeguards to ensure that "initial content" (i.e. Role Collections, etc) that gets created when a service/application that is subscribed is installed onto the BTP Subaccount that it would be excluded from "target system" SCIM processing. The only valid 'group' SCIM process that should be allowed on the target systems like SAP BTP XS Advanced UAA (Cloud Foundry)) is the 'group to user' assignment. So far, I have not been able to identify any target system Transformation code that only allows just 'group to user' assignment processing. 

 

If anyone else have come across this issue and found a workable solution I would be very interest to know, what was done.

Regards,

Steve

entry.title: Successfactors emplo

I imported new employees using the New Employee Import template and completed only the initial/basic employee import successfully. At this stage, I intentionally stopped before proceeding with additional imports such as:

  • Job Information
  • Employment Information
  • Compensation Information
  • Email Information

I wanted to first troubleshoot and validate the initial employee creation before moving further.

Current issue:
After the basic import, I can see related records such as job codes, but when searching employees in SuccessFactors, the employee names themselves are not appearing correctly in search results.

In some cases:

  • the employee ID exists,
  • the job code appears,
  • but the employee profile/name is not searchable as expected.

I am trying to understand:

  1. Is this expected behavior if only the basic import is completed?
  2. Are there specific entities/imports required before the employee becomes fully searchable in EC?
  3. Could this be related to missing User ID / Person ID External / Employment linkage?
  4. Is there a recommended sequence for completing new hire imports in EC to avoid partial employee creation issues?

Any guidance would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you!

entry.title: How to Deploy a Custom Fiori App on SAP BTP and Add a Tile in SAP Build Work Zone
entry.content:
How to Deploy a Custom Fiori App on SAP BTP and Add a Tile in SAP Build Work Zone

Introduction

If you've built a custom Fiori application on SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud using RAP (ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model) and want to expose it as a tile in SAP Build Work Zone, this tutorial walks you through the complete process — from deployment in SAP Business Application Studio (BAS) to the final working tile in Work Zone.
This guide assumes you already have:
  • A working RAP-based OData service on S/4HANA Public Cloud
  • A Fiori application project in BAS that consumes this service
  • Access to SAP Build Work Zone (standard edition)
  • The app accessible via ADT preview

By the end of this tutorial, your app will be accessible as a tile in your Work Zone site, sitting alongside other tiles like My Inbox.

Architecture Overview

SAP Business Application Studio (BAS)
        │
        │  Deploy to ABAP Repository
        ▼
SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud 
   ├── OData Service (Service Binding)
   ├── IAM App (External App) + LADI
   ├── Business Catalog
   └── Business Role
        │
        │  Semantic Object + Action
        ▼
SAP Build Work Zone (BTP)
   └── Tile → resolves intent → opens S/4 app via SSO

Prerequisites

  • SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud system with developer access
  • SAP Business Application Studio (BAS) with a destination configured to your S/4 system
  • SAP Build Work Zone subscription on your BTP subaccount
  • A working Fiori app project in BAS (currently accessible via ADT preview only)
  • Both BTP and S/4 trusting the same SAP Cloud Identity Services (IAS) tenant for SSO

Step 1: Configure crossNavigation in manifest.json

Before deploying, your Fiori app needs to declare how it will be launched from the Fiori Launchpad. This is done via the crossNavigation/inbounds section in manifest.json.
Open webapp/manifest.json and add the following inside the "sap.app" section:
"crossNavigation": {
  "inbounds": {
    "intent1": {
      "semanticObject": "ZObjectName",
      "action": "display",
      "signature": {
        "parameters": {},
        "additionalParameters": "allowed"
      },
      "title": "Opportunity Analytics",
      "subtitle": "",
      "icon": "sap-icon://opportunity"
    }
  }
}
Key points:
  • The semanticObject and action together form the intent (#ZObjectName-display) that the Fiori Launchpad and Work Zone will use to navigate to your app.
  • Choose a meaningful semantic object name (prefix with Z for custom).
  • The title here will appear on the tile.
  • To browse available icons for your tile, see the Icon Explorer . 
Important: Also ensure your i18n property in sap.app uses the simple string format:
"i18n": "i18n/i18n.properties"
Do NOT use the object format: { "bundleName": "..." }

Note: The S/4HANA Cloud LADI converter cannot process the object format for i18n. Use the simple string format instead. For more details on this issue, see:
UI5 CLI - GitHub Issue #432 
SAP Help Portal | SAP Online Help 

Your manifest.json should look like: 

Step 2: Ensure i18n.properties File Exists

Make sure the file webapp/i18n/i18n.properties exists and contains at minimum:
appTitle=App Title
appDescription=A SAP Fiori Application
Without this file, the LADI creation will fail with the error: "File is not a 'properties' file for texts."

Step 3: Configure Deployment to ABAP

In BAS, configure the deployment target. There are two ways to do this:
Option A: Via Application Info (UI-based)
  1. Right-click your project and select "Open Application Info"
  2. Click the "Deploy" option
  3. Fill in the deployment configuration fields
Option B: Via terminal command
npm run deploy-config
This launches the same configuration wizard in the terminal.
 
Both approaches will prompt you to fill in the following fields:

FieldValue
Target TypeABAP
DestinationYour S/4 destination 
SAPUI5 ABAP RepositoryA unique name, max 15 chars (eg: ZREPO_NAME)
Deployment DescriptionAnalytics page for opportunity tracker
PackageYour ABAP package
Transport RequestYour transport request (or leave empty for $TMP)

This generates a ui5-deploy.yaml file:

Step 4: Deploy the Application

Run the deployment from the BAS terminal:
npm run deploy
This builds the app and deploys it to the S/4HANA Cloud ABAP repository.

What to look for in the output:

SAPUI5 Application has been uploaded and registered successfully
Updated LADIs for UI5 Repository "", package "", transport ""
Inbound intent1 (ZObjectName-display) was converted to LADI ZREPO_NAME_UI5R
Launchpad App Descriptor Item ZREPO_NAME_UI5R was updated
Deployment Successful.

For example:

 

If you see warnings like:
  • "No LADI was created as no inbound is defined in manifest.json" → You're missing the crossNavigation block
  • "Could not convert UI5 Descriptor to LADI(s) / File is not a 'properties' file for texts" → Change i18n to the simple string format

Step 5: Create an IAM App (External App) in S/4

After deployment, the LADI exists on the system but isn't yet linked to any access control. You need to create a new IAM App to hold it and grant access to the OData service.
  1. Open your S/4HANA Cloud system
  2. Search for and open the "Create IAM App" application
  3. Create a new IAM App with:
    • App Type: External App
    • IAM App ID: A meaningful name 
  4. In the "Services" section:
    • Add your OData service
    • This grants the app authorization to call the backend service
  5. In the "Fiori Launchpad App Descr Items" section:
    • Add the LADI ID: ZREPO_NAME_UI5R
    • This enables Launchpad intent navigation
  6. Save and publish

 

 

Why a separate IAM App?
Your OData service already has its own IAM App (type: Custom Inbound Service App) for API access. The External App IAM App is specifically for Launchpad navigation.

Step 6: Add IAM App to a Business Catalog

  • Open Maintain Business Catalogs (or create a new catalog in ADT)
  • Open or create your catalog 
  • Add your new External App IAM App to this catalog
  • Publish the catalog

 

 

Step 7: Assign Business Catalog to a Business Role

  • Open Maintain Business Roles
  • Open the Business Role assigned to your users
  • Go to Assigned Business Catalogs
  • Add your catalog 
  • Save

Step 8: Verify on S/4 Fiori Launchpad

Before adding the tile in Work Zone, verify the app works natively on S/4:
https://<your-system>.lab.s4hana.cloud.sap/ui#ZObjectName-display
Your app should open without errors.
If you get "navigation target could not be resolved", double-check:
  • The LADI is added to the IAM App
  • The IAM App is in a Business Catalog
  • The Business Catalog is in your user's Business Role
  • You've logged out and back in after making changes

Step 9: Add the Tile in SAP Build Work Zone

Now that the app works on S/4 via intent navigation, add it to Work Zone:
  • Open SAP Build Work Zone administration
  • Go to Content Manager
  • Create a new App
In the Navigation tab:
  • Semantic Object: ZObjectName (same as the one in manifest.json)
  • Action: display
In the Visualization tab:
  • Title, subtitle, icon as desired
  • Save the app

Step 10: Assign to Role and Group

  • In Content Manager → Roles: Open your role and assign the new app
  • In Content Manager → Groups: Open your group (e.g., "SBPA") and add the app
  • Ensure the role is mapped to a Role Collection that your users have

Step 11: Verify the Tile in Work Zone

Open your Work Zone site.
Your new tile should appear in the designated group/section.
Click it — the app opens seamlessly via SSO (since both BTP and S/4 trust the same IAS tenant).

Troubleshooting

Issue CauseFix
"No LADI was created" during deployMissing crossNavigation/inbounds in manifest.jsonAdd the crossNavigation block and redeploy
"Could not convert UI5 Descriptor to LADI(s)"i18n uses object formatChange to "i18n": "i18n/i18n.properties"
"Navigation target could not be resolved"LADI not linked to IAM App, or missing catalog/role assignmentAdd LADI to External App IAM App → Business Catalog → Business Role
403 Forbidden / UCON blockedTrying to access direct BSP URLUse Launchpad URL (/ui#SemanticObject-action)
Login prompt (username/password) in Work ZoneURL uses -api endpoint, or SSO not configuredUse non-api URL; ensure both BTP and S/4 trust the same IAS tenant
"Destination https://https://..." errorDouble protocol in URLRemove the extra https:// prefix

Key Takeaways

  • crossNavigation/inbounds in manifest.json is what creates the LADI during deployment.
  • i18n must use the simple string format ("i18n": "i18n/i18n.properties") for the LADI converter to work.
  • A separate IAM App of type External App is needed for the Launchpad tile.
  • The chain must be complete: LADI → IAM App → Business Catalog → Business Role → User
  • In Work Zone, use semantic object + action — not URL tiles.
  • SSO works automatically when both BTP and S/4 trust the same IAS tenant.

Conclusion

The path from "app works in ADT preview" to "tile in Work Zone" requires a clear understanding of the deployment chain:
  • BAS deploys the UI and creates the LADI
  • The IAM App links the LADI to access control
  • Work Zone resolves the semantic intent
Each piece is simple on its own, but missing any single step results in confusing errors.
Once this flow is established, adding future apps follows the same pattern — making it repeatable and scalable across your organization.

Tags

SAP S/4HANA Cloud, SAP Build Work Zone, SAP Business Application Studio, Fiori, RAP, ABAP Cloud, LADI, IAM App, Tile Configuration

entry.title: Business Data Validation in SAP Transformations: Leveraging LTVF to Strengthen Auditability
entry.content:
This blog explores the role of SAP DMLT (Data Management & Landscape Transformation) Business Data Validation (BDV) service leveraging Landscape Transformation Validation Framework (LTVF) in strengthening auditability, governance, and business data integrity during SAP transformation initiatives.

As enterprises increasingly adopt SAP S/4HANA through system consolidations, selective data transitions and undergoes transformations such as mergers, acquisitions and divestures, the complexity of validating migrated business data has significantly increased. Traditional manual reconciliation methods are often insufficient to provide scalable, repeatable, and audit-ready validation evidence.

This blog talks about how LTVF introduces automated reconciliation, traceability, and repeatable validation controls to support financial integrity assurance, regulatory compliance, and enterprise governance throughout the transformation lifecycle.

Introduction

Large SAP transformation programs such as:

  • SAP S/4HANA migrations
  • System Consolidations
  • Carve-outs
  • Mergers, Acquisitions, Divestures
  • Selective Data Transitions

involve movement and transformation of massive amounts of business-critical data. During these projects, organizations must ensure that:

  • Data integrity is maintained
  • Financial balances remain accurate
  • Business processes continue correctly
  • Compliance requirements are met

One of the major concerns in such projects is auditability. Auditors and governance teams need evidence proving that data was migrated correctly without loss, duplication, or unauthorized modification.

To address these challenges, within SAP DMLT-led transformation engagements, LTVF acts as a centralized business data validation framework that supports transformation assurance activities across technical migration, business reconciliation, and governance work streams.

LTVF provides systematic controls, reconciliation reports, and audit-ready evidence throughout the transformation lifecycle.

Understanding LTVF

Traditional reconciliation approaches in SAP systems often rely on fragmented spreadsheet-based comparisons and manually executed sampling techniques. Such approaches introduce risks related to human error, inconsistent validation logic, incomplete coverage, and limited audit traceability. Transformation projects are typically high-risk in terms of data because they involve critical enterprise data such as:

  • Financial records
  • Logistics data
  • Customer data
  • Utility billing data
  • Asset information
  • Transactional history
  • and much more

LTVF stands for Landscape Transformation Validation Framework. It is a validation and reconciliation framework developed to automate:

  • Data Comparison
  • Migration Validation
  • Reconciliation
  • and Audit Support activities

LTVF compares source and target systems to verify that transformed data remains accurate and complete after migration.

The framework reduces dependency on:

  • Manual Spreadsheet validation
  • Ad hoc reconciliation methods
  • Time-consuming audit preparation activities

Refer below SAP Notes for detailed information about SAP DMLT BDV Service and LTVF framework: -

https://me.sap.com/notes/3271210

https://me.sap.com/notes/3271502

 

Importance of Auditing in SAP Transformation Projects

SAP transformation projects affect core enterprise operations. Even a small data inconsistency can create:

  • Financial inaccuracies
  • Operational disruptions
  • Regulatory violations
  • Customer service issues

Auditors therefore require evidence that:

  • All records were migrated
  • Balances reconcile correctly
  • Data transformations followed approved rules
  • Internal controls were maintained
  • And much more

Traditional manual validation approaches are often insufficient because:

  • SAP systems contain millions of records
  • Multiple systems may be involved
  • Validations must be repeated across test cycles
  • Audit evidence must be traceable

LTVF addresses these challenges by introducing automation and governance into the validation process.

Role of LTVF in the Auditing Process

1.) Automated Data Reconciliation

One of the primary audit requirements during migration projects is reconciliation between source and target systems.

LTVF automates reconciliation activities with:

  • Volume validation for example Record counts
  • Financial reconciliation for example GL/FICA balances
  • Field-Level validation for example Address/tax data
  • Aggregated validation for example Billing/Balances totals
  • Validation of document totals, Accounts receivable/payables totals
  • Exception Validation for example Missing/Unexpected records

LTVF identifies the discrepancy automatically and shows the errors which can be downloaded as spreadsheet for future reference.

This helps auditors confirm whether records were:

  • Intentionally excluded
  • Incorrectly transformed
  • or accidentally lost

Examples below depict a successful record count and all field level validations for vendor master, material document list and material master respectively.

2.) Audit Trail and Traceability

An important component of auditing is maintaining a complete audit trail.

LTVF maintains:

  • Validation execution logs
  • Reconciliation history
  • Rule configurations logs
  • Comparison results

This provides traceability for:

  • When validations occurred
  • Which objects were validated
  • What errors were identified

Auditors can therefore independently verify:

  • Migration completeness
  • Validation coverage
  • Issue resolution activities

This might be important for:

  • SOX compliance
  • Financial audits
  • Internal control assessments
  • Regulatory reporting.

Example below depicts a configuration rule REPLACE which was applied while comparison of Source and Target system data

3.) Validation of Financial Integrity

Financial integrity is one of the most critical audit areas in SAP migrations.

LTVF validates whether:

  • Financial balances match
  • Postings remain consistent
  • Totals are preserved
  • Transactional relationships are intact

Typical financial validations include:

  • General Ledger balances
  • Open item reconciliation
  • Asset accounting balances
  • Tax totals
  • Profitability reporting figures

Example

During a system consolidation or S4 HANA migration project:

  • source company codes may be merged into a single target company code.

LTVF validates:

  • Debit and credit balances
  • Account mappings
  • Historical document consistency

This provides confidence to finance auditors that the migration did not introduce financial discrepancies.

Examples below depicts financial statement reconciliation and GL account balances validation between an SAP ECC and S4 HANA system: -

Auditors can review these differences/missing records and can maintain documentation for whether these errors are expected and accepted formally. Customers can also use LTVF sign-off functionality to maintain the documentation for sign-off logs.

4.) Error Identification and Resolution

No migration project is entirely error-free. LTVF helps to identify for example following inconsistencies: -

  • Missing records
  • Duplicate entries
  • Invalid mappings
  • Inconsistent master data
  • Transformation failures

The framework categorizes and reports errors so that:

  • Migration teams can investigate issues
  • Business teams can validate corrections
  • Auditors can review the issues and corrections

This creates a controlled error management process.

Auditors can verify:

  • whether issues were resolved
  • whether approvals were documented
  • whether residual risks were accepted formally

5.) Repeatable Validation Across Test Cycles

SAP transformation projects usually include:

  • Multiple mock migrations (Test cycles)
  • Dress rehearsals
  • Production cutover

Manual validations are time consuming and become extremely difficult to repeat consistently across cycles with increased efforts.

LTVF allows validation scenarios to be reused repeatedly with the same rules and logic.

This ensures:

  • Consistency
  • Standardization
  • Repeatable audit controls

From an auditing perspective, repeatable validations demonstrate process maturity and control effectiveness.

6.) Support for Governance and Compliance

Modern organizations must comply with various governance frameworks and regulations such as:

  • SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley)
  • GDPR
  • Financial reporting standards
  • Internal audit requirements

LTVF supports governance by:

  • Enforcing structured validation procedures
  • Maintaining documented evidence
  • Supporting segregation of duties

For example:

  • Migration teams execute transformations
  • Business users review validation results
  • Auditors independently review evidence

This separation improves compliance and reduces operational risk.

7.) Reduction of Manual Audit Effort

Before automated frameworks like LTVF, audit preparation from customer side often required:

  • Manual extraction of SAP tables
  • Spreadsheet reconciliations
  • Custom SQL comparisons
  • Extensive business involvement

These activities were:

  • time-consuming
  • error-prone
  • and difficult to scale

LTVF significantly reduces manual effort through:

  • Automated comparison rules
  • Centralized reporting
  • Reusable validation templates
  • Standardized reconciliation procedures

As a result:

  • Audits become faster
  • Migration quality improves
  • Operational risk decreases

Real-World Scenario

Consider a utility company migrating from three SAP IS-U systems into a single SAP S/4HANA Utilities environment.

Critical audit concerns may include:

  • whether all customers migrated
  • whether utility billing history remained intact
  • whether FICA balances reconciled
  • whether device and meter relationships were preserved

LTVF can validate:

  • Contract account counts
  • Billing document totals
  • Payment balances
  • Business partner relationships
  • and many more

The generated results become formal evidence for:

  • Project governance
  • Go-Live approval
  • and external audit reviews

In summary, LTVF can validate the integrity of highly interconnected utility objects such as Business Partners, Contract Accounts, Installations, Devices, Meter Reading Documents, Billing Documents, and FICA open items.

Key Benefits of LTVF in Auditing

Improved Accuracy: - Automated validations reduce human error.

Faster Audit Readiness: - Audit evidence is generated continuously during the project.

Better Traceability: - All validations and errors are logged systematically.

Higher Confidence in Migration Quality: - Organizations gain assurance that critical business data remains intact.

Reduced Operational Risk: - Issues are identified early before production Go-Live.\

Conclusion

As enterprise transformation programs continue to increase in complexity, automated business data validation frameworks are becoming critical components of transformation governance and audit readiness. SAP DMLT’s LTVF enables organizations to transition from reactive reconciliation approaches to proactive, controlled, and repeatable validation methodologies that support both operational confidence and regulatory assurance.

Through automated reconciliation, traceable validation processes, and repeatable controls, LTVF enables organizations to provide reliable audit evidence throughout the migration lifecycle.

As SAP landscapes become increasingly complex and enterprises move toward SAP S/4HANA transformations, frameworks such as LTVF are becoming essential components of:

  • Migration governance
  • Compliance assurance
  • Financial integrity validation
  • Enterprise audit readiness

LTVF not only improves migration quality but also helps organizations reduce audit risk and build confidence in large-scale digital transformation initiatives.

Refer SAP Note https://me.sap.com/notes/3755556/E for summary of LTVF audit aspects.

entry.title: Vietnam Financial Statement Output Shows Blank Figures After 2602 Release
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I'm facing an issue with the Vietnam Financial Statement generated via the Run Statutory Reports app after the 2602 release.

When I run the report with an action date before 02/2026, the output is generated correctly with all balances displayed. However, when I run the same report with an action date after 02/2026, the financial statement is generated but all balance figures are blank.

I have tested this issue in several environments:

In the first environment, only the Balance Sheet has the issue.

  • Action date: January 2026 → report generated correctly 

  • Action date: May 2026 → all balances are blank 

 

In the second environment, the behavior is different:

 

Balance Sheet in action date: May 2026 → generated correctly

Income Statement in action date: May 2026 → all balances are blank 

Has anyone faced a similar issue after the 2602 release, or is there any SAP Note/configuration that should be checked?

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Running KILL
entry.content:
The process you see 'Running' should be your current dbisql process but not the mentioned 'hanging/zombie' process from gdb.
The Error '6104 - You cannot use KILL to kill your own process' will raise when you are trying to kill your current process.

You can verify your current spid by execute this command in dbisql:
select @@spid

To verify if there are hanging process in ASE server you can run:
select * from master..syslogshold
go

Are there spids returned? Check the timestamp to find which corresponds to the process of when gdb connected ASE. Then try to kill that spid.

entry.title: SAP CPQ: Involved Parties の Sold-To Party 名を Quote List に表示する方法
entry.content:
概要

SAP CPQ のデフォルトの Quote List には、Involved Parties で設定した Sold-To Party (受注先) の Business Partner 名が列として表示されません。 本記事では、Quote カスタムフィールドと Global Script を組み合わせることで、この情報を Quote List 上に表示する手順を解説します。

対象バージョン: SAP CPQ Quote 2.0 エンジン (CPQ 2605 以降で動作確認)

アーキテクチャ概要

実現方法の全体像は以下の通りです。

コンポーネント 役割

Quote カスタムフィールド SoldToPartyNameSold-To Party 名を格納する FreeForm フィールド
Quote List Settings上記フィールドを列として Quote List に表示する設定
Global Script SoldToPartyNameInvolved Parties 変更時に Sold-To Party 名を読み取りカスタムフィールドへ書き込む IronPython スクリプト

図 1: 完成形 — Quote List の "Sold-To Party" 列に Business Partner 名 "Alpha Center" が表示されている

 

手順

Step 1: Quote カスタムフィールドを作成する

Setup > Quotes > Quote Fields を開き、「Custom Fields」タブで新規フィールドを作成します。

設定項目 値

NameSoldToPartyName
TypeFreeForm
Default LabelSold-To Party
Calculations are run「On Involved Parties Changed」にチェックを入れる
Formula (Implementation)空欄のまま ※後述の Global Script で値を設定するため

 

図 2: カスタムフィールド設定画面 — Type: FreeForm、"On Involved Parties Changed" にチェック、Formula は空欄

Step 2: Quote List Settings に列を追加する

Setup > Quotes > Quote List Settings を開き、「Columns Layout」セクションで手順 1 で作成した SoldToPartyName フィールドを選択して追加します。

図 3: Quote List Settings の Columns Layout に "Sold-To Party" を追加した状態

ポイント: 列ラベルはフィールドの「Default Label」で設定した文字列 (Sold-To Party) が使われます。ユーザータイプ別にラベルを変えたい場合は「User Types By Labels」で設定してください。

Step 3: Global Script を作成する

Setup > Develop > Global Scripts を開き、「Add Script」で新規スクリプトを作成します。

設定項目 値

NameSoldToPartyName（任意）
System ID一意な ID（例: SoldToPartyName_cpq）※自動設定
Activeチェックを入れる

「Script Code」欄に以下のコードを入力し、Save してください。

# Sold-To Party の Business Partner 名を取得し、カスタムフィールドに書き込む
soldToName = ""
for party in context.Quote.GetInvolvedParties():
    if party.PartnerFunctionName == "Sold-to party":
        soldToName = party.Name or ""
        break
context.Quote["SoldToPartyName"] = soldToName

図 4: Global Script のコード入力画面 — Quote 2.0 API を使用したスクリプト

スクリプトの解説

コード 説明

context.Quote.GetInvolvedParties()Quote 2.0 API。Involved Parties に登録された全パーティを返す
party.PartnerFunctionNameパートナー機能名（例: "Sold-to party"）
party.NameBusiness Partner の名称（例: "Alpha Center"）
context.Quote["SoldToPartyName"]Quote 2.0 でカスタムフィールドに値をセットする記法

 

Save 後、ページをリロードして Events タブを開きます。

図 5: Global Script の Events タブ — "On Involved Parties Changed" イベントにチェックを入れる

「On Involved Parties Changed」にチェックを入れ、Save してください。これにより Involved Parties が変更されるたびにこのスクリプトが自動実行されます。

Step 4: 動作確認

  1. 任意の Quote を開き、「Involved parties」タブに遷移します。
  2. Sold-to party として任意の Business Partner を設定し、Save します。
  3. Quote List に戻ると、「Sold-To Party」列に Business Partner 名が表示されます。

図 6: Quote の Involved Parties タブ — ここで Sold-to party を設定することでスクリプトがトリガーされる

図 7: Quote List での確認結果 — Quote 02080027 の "Sold-To Party" 列に "Alpha Center" が表示されている

まとめ

本記事では、SAP CPQ Quote 2.0 環境において Involved Parties の Sold-To Party 名を Quote List に表示する方法を解説しました。要点は以下の 3 点です。

  1. FreeForm カスタムフィールドを作成し、Quote List の列に追加する
  2. Global Script に Quote 2.0 API (context.Quote.GetInvolvedParties()) を使ったスクリプトを記述する
  3. Global Script の Events タブで「On Involved Parties Changed」を有効化する

ポイント: 同じ手法を応用すれば、Ship-To Party や Bill-To Party など他のパートナー機能名を Quote List に表示することも可能です。party.PartnerFunctionName の比較文字列を変更するだけで対応できます。

参考リンク

  • SAP CPQ Scripting Interfaces ドキュメント (2605)
  • SAP CPQ Help Portal

entry.title: Re: How do upgrade SAP Business Apllication Studio Plan f...
entry.content: Please note that if the service is already subscribed, updating the plan should be from the "Instances and Subscriptions" section: Select the ellipsis at the end of the subscription row, and from the menu, click Update. In any case, do not unsubscribe and resubscribe, as that will result with lost of all existing dev spaces data.

entry.title: Unable to run Hardware Configuration Check Tool (HWCCT)
entry.content:
Dear SAP Team,

We are currently attempting to perform hardware validation checks for our SAP HANA system. However, we are facing issues with both the SAP Hardware Configuration Check Tool (HWCCT) and some of the required OS-level validation commands.

Details are as follows:

1. We attempted to execute the HWCCT utility using:
python hwcct.py

but received the following error:
"python: can't open file 'hwcct.py': [Errno 2] No such file or directory"

2. We also searched the server for the HWCCT utility using:
find /usr/sap -iname "hwcct"

but no HWCCT files were found on the system.

numactl --hardware

returns:

Could you please assist us with:

entry.title: Re: Migration fixed asset nbv less than 1
entry.content:
Hi,

the easiest way in this case is to set the indicator "Negative Values allowed" to "Yes" in the depreciation area settings of the asset master record.

As you use "memo values" your takeover data is not consistent and violates the configuration settings. Thus the error message is justified. But setting above indicator your "overrule" this setting specifically for this asset in its valuation settings.

Best Regards,
Markus

entry.title: SAP Business Network: How to choose the right account or subscription for your business
entry.content:
A few days ago we brought suppliers together to address a question that comes up time and again — which account or subscription actually fits your business? Here is what we covered.

Whether you are new to SAP Business Network or already transacting, you may recognise and even relate to some of these questions:

  • What type of account or subscription do I need?
  • What are the limitations of a Standard vs Enterprise account?
  • How much do subscriptions cost?
  • When should I upgrade?
  • How do I decide if I am not sure how much business I will get from my customers on the network?

We aim to bring clarity to exactly these questions.

What are the options? 

Suppliers have 3 options when it comes down to it: 

  1. Standard account - free account. 
  2. Enterprise account – consists of various subscription tiers and transaction thresholds.
  3. Promote subscription:  a value-added add on subscription to boost sales via higher search ranking, network catalogs and access to SAP Business Network Discovery leads and RFx opportunities.

How do you decide?

The decision process should be based on these factors:

  1. Your number of customers along with current or projected transaction volumes
  2. Your business goals - as part of a growth strategy and new customer acquisition.
  3. Your operational needs - system integration, analytics, compliance etc.

As a rule of thumb, Standard account is a good start for low-volumes or single-buyer scenarios. 

On the other hand, suppliers with higher-volume or those seeking integration, dedicated support and data insights will get more value from an Enterprise account.

Is it worth it?

Independent IDC research reports a 416% three-year ROI for suppliers on the network, with benefits including revenue growth, stronger governance and compliance, and improved cross-sell/up-sell effectiveness.

Next Steps:

  • Explore account and subscription options on our Community for Suppliers page
  • Watch the replay this 30-min session led by our supplier-dedicated NEAT team and don’t miss the open Q&A part at the end for more insights shared with fellow suppliers. 
  • Join the next webinar on how to 'Unlock the Full Power of SAP Business Network: A Deep Dive into Enterprise Account Benefits' June 10th at 11 AM CEST, 11am EST , 12.30 AEDT or in other languages here.
  • Already have an Enterprise account and want to talk through specific challenges?  Feel free to contact our NEAT team for a free consultation.

In conclusion, no one size fits all and that is exactly the point.  These options exist to better suit suppliers and meet them where they are at, whatever their challenges, setup or ambitions. 

What factors matter most to you when choosing the right setup on SAP Business Network — and have you found the right fit yet? Share your thoughts in the comments.

entry.title: Re: How to import a Groovy class reference in Resources->...
entry.content:
Hi @Integration12345 ,

Great question! When you move a POGO class from a Local Script to a Common Script Collection, the import path changes because the classpath namespace shifts.

When the class is in a Local Script, the import works as:
import src.main.resources.script.ClassName

However, when moved to a Common Script Collection, the classpath includes the Script Collection's Artifact ID. So the import path becomes:
import src.main.resources.script.<ScriptCollectionArtifactID>.ClassName

For example, if your Script Collection Artifact ID is 'CommonScripts':
import src.main.resources.script.CommonScripts.ClassName

A few things to double-check:

1. Artifact ID vs Display Name - Make sure you're using the Artifact ID of the Script Collection (set during creation), not its display name. You can verify this in your CPI tenant under the Script Collection details.

2. File and Class Name Match - The Groovy file name must exactly match the class name (e.g., ClassName.groovy containing class ClassName).

3. Add Script Collection as Dependency - In your iFlow, go to References → Script Collection and ensure the Common Script Collection is listed as a dependency. Without this, the import will fail even with the correct path.

4. Redeploy Both Artifacts - After making changes, redeploy the Script Collection first, then the iFlow. CPI caches scripts aggressively, so a redeploy of just the iFlow may not be enough.

Hope this helps!

Regards,

Naveen

entry.title: Auto-Populating Transfer of Control Dates for 5MQ Intercompany Sales — Billing Date as the Source
entry.content:
Background

In a previous blog post, one scenario of auto-populating the External Actual Transfer of Control Date was discussed. Today we continue with another variation — this time in the context of 5MQ (Sell from Stock with Valuated Stock in Transit), where the date source and trigger are different.

The business requirement here has two parts:

  1. When an outbound delivery is created, default the External Planned Transfer of Control Date to 9999-12-31 — this keeps the POD window open indefinitely and prevents it from expiring before billing takes place.
  2. When the delivery is saved after billing, automatically populate the External Actual Transfer of Control Date from the corresponding Billing Document Date (posting date of the billing document).

Both fields are sap:updatable="false" in the standard OData API (API_OUTBOUND_DELIVERY_SRV), so BAdI-based extensibility is the only viable path.

The two fields in question on the outbound delivery Shipment tab:

  • External Planned Transfer of Control Date (INTCOEXTPLNDTRANSFOFCTRLDTETME)
  • External Actual Transfer of Control Date (INTCOEXTACTLTRANSFOFCTRLDTETME)

Enhancement Points

BAdI 1 — LE_SHP_MODIFY_HEAD (Key User Extensibility)

Purpose: Default the Planned Transfer of Control Date to 9999-12-31 at delivery creation.

Entry point: Custom Logic app (#CustomLogic-develop) — Key User Extensibility, no transport required.

Implementation:

 
delivery_document_out = delivery_document_in.

DATA lv_max_ts TYPE timestamp.
lv_max_ts = '99991231000000'.

IF delivery_document_in-intcoextplndtransfofctrldtetme IS INITIAL.
  delivery_document_out-intcoextplndtransfofctrldtetme = lv_max_ts.
ENDIF.

This BAdI operates under a FUNCTIONAL transactional contract — no database reads allowed, which is fine here since we only need a field assignment.

BAdI 2 — LE_SHP_SAVE_DOCUMENT_PREPARE (Key User Extensibility)

Purpose: Populate the Actual Transfer of Control Date from the billing document date when the delivery is saved.

Entry point: Custom Logic app (#CustomLogic-develop) — same as above.

Why not LE_SHP_MODIFY_HEAD for this? LE_SHP_MODIFY_HEAD enforces a FUNCTIONAL contract at runtime — any SELECT statement raises a transactional contract violation error. LE_SHP_SAVE_DOCUMENT_PREPARE does not have this restriction and allows reads against standard CDS views.

Implementation:

 
delivery_document_out = delivery_document_in.

DATA lv_max_ts TYPE timestamp.
lv_max_ts = '99991231000000'.
delivery_document_out-intcoextplndtransfofctrldtetme = lv_max_ts.

CHECK delivery_document_in-shippingpoint = '1311' OR
      delivery_document_in-shippingpoint = '1320'.

CHECK delivery_document_in-intcoextactltransfofctrldtetme IS INITIAL.

SELECT SINGLE t~billingdocumentdate
  FROM i_billingdocument AS t
    INNER JOIN i_billingdocumentitem AS i
      ON i~billingdocument = t~billingdocument
  WHERE i~referencesddocument         = @delivery_document_in-deliverydocument
    AND i~referencesddocumentcategory = 'J'
    AND t~billingdocumentiscancelled  = ''
  INTO @DATA(lv_billing_date).

IF sy-subrc = 0 AND lv_billing_date IS NOT INITIAL.
  DATA(lv_date_str) = |{ lv_billing_date DATE = RAW }|.
  DATA lv_timestamp TYPE timestamp.
  lv_timestamp = |{ lv_date_str }000000|.
  delivery_document_out-intcoextactltransfofctrldtetme = lv_timestamp.
ENDIF.

Design notes:

  • Scoped to specific Shipping Points — adjust shippingpoint values to match your configuration
  • CHECK IS INITIAL on the Actual date ensures idempotency — the date is only set once and won't be overwritten on subsequent saves
  • ReferenceSDDocumentCategory = 'J' filters for outbound deliveries in the document flow
  • Date-to-timestamp conversion uses string template syntax; CONVERT DATE ... INTO TIME STAMP is not available in the Custom Logic environment
  • No custom tables or Z CDS views required — standard CDS views I_BillingDocument and I_BillingDocumentItem are sufficient

Test Result

Outbound delivery 80000325, Shipping Point 1311/1320, with an existing billing document dated 05/28/2026:

before

after 

The Actual Transfer of Control Date matches the billing document date. The Planned date remains 9999-12-31 as expected.

One thing need to mention, after billing is completed and the invoice has a posting date, it is still necessary to manually trigger an outbound delivery update in order for the update to be successful.

Summary

Requirement BAdI Contract Approach

Default Planned date to 9999-12-31LE_SHP_MODIFY_HEADFUNCTIONALDirect field assignment
Populate Actual date from billingLE_SHP_SAVE_DOCUMENT_PREPARENo restrictionSELECT on I_BillingDocument + I_BillingDocumentItem

Both implementations are maintained entirely in the Custom Logic Fiori app — no developer extensibility, no transport requests.

The features covered in this article are based on SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition 2602, please refer to the latest information for changes in subsequent versions.

Hope you LIKE it if it addresses your issue. After that, please feel free to comment after following my account and I will reply ASAP.

entry.title: Re: Read Multiple Attachments in CPI
entry.content:
Hi @mashfaq ,

The issue is happening because the Mail adapter is still processing the complete attachment map instead of a single attachment during each loop iteration.

From your properties:

AttachmentCount = 2
AttachmentList = {39AHD.pdf=..., 0395_20260518135718.102_X.pdf=...}

the loop is iterating correctly, but the attachment handling inside the loop is not removing or isolating one attachment at a time.

To send exactly one attachment per email, you need to:

  1. Read one attachment from the AttachmentList

  2. Set only that attachment to the message

  3. Remove previous attachments before the next iteration

Usually this is handled with a Groovy script inside the Looping Process Call.

Example approach:

import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message
import javax.activation.DataHandler

def Message processData(Message message) {

    def attachments = message.getAttachments()

    // Clear all existing attachments first
    message.getAttachmentWrapperObjects().clear()

    // Pick single attachment
    def firstKey = attachments.keySet().iterator().next()

    DataHandler dh = attachments.get(firstKey)

    // Add only one attachment
    message.addAttachmentObject(firstKey, dh)

    return message
}

Also verify:

  • The Looping Process Call is decrementing AttachmentCount

  • The processed attachment is removed from AttachmentList

  • The Mail adapter is not configured to automatically include all message attachments

Most importantly, after each iteration you should remove the processed attachment from the map; otherwise CPI keeps reusing the full attachment collection in every loop.

That is why you currently receive either:

  • all attachments in every email, or

  • empty emails after attachments are cleared.

Regards,

Naveen

entry.title: Re: France E-Invoicing Set up from S4H public Cloud Via S...
entry.content:
Hi @Jyothi5 ,

This looks more like a system limitation/current SAP-supported setup rather than a configuration issue from your side.

At the moment, SAP supports the France E-Invoicing scenario only through the cf-eu02 BTP region. So, if your subaccount is in cf-eu10, the France DRC process may not work there currently.

The note about the “qualification system” simply means that SAP currently allows customers to test the integration with the French tax authority’s test environment only. The actual production/live connection is not yet fully available.

So in simple terms:

  • cf-eu02 is currently the required/supported region for this scenario.
  • Only testing connectivity is supported for now.
  • This appears to be an SAP platform restriction/current rollout status, not an issue with your implementation.

Regards,

Naveen

entry.title: Unlocking Fiori Applications for Process Order Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
When working with Fiori applications related to process orders — such as Create Process Order (C0R1), Change Process Order (C0R2), Display Process Order (C0R3), and Confirm Process Order Phase (C0R6N) — you may notice that these apps and their associated business catalogs are not readily available in your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition system. This can be puzzling, especially when scope items like 42N, 3UK, 77G, or 3OK are already active.

Why the Applications Are Missing

The key lies in understanding the foundational scope item BJ8 (Make-to-Stock Production – Process Manufacturing Based on Process Order).

  • The technical business catalog SAP_SCM_BC_PROC_ORD_MGMT_MC (Process Order Management) is delivered through BJ8.

  • Specialized scope items such as 42N, 3UK, 77G, or 3OK depend on BJ8 to establish the underlying process manufacturing framework.

  • If BJ8 is not activated in your Starter environment, the catalog cannot be pulled into your assigned business roles, which explains why the apps are missing.

How to Enable the Applications

To make these applications available for your workshops or productive use, follow these steps:

  • Activate Scope Item BJ8 Once BJ8 is active, the catalog SAP_SCM_BC_PROC_ORD_MGMT_MC will be included in the standard business roles.

  • Assign Business Catalogs If you are using a customized business role, ensure that the catalog is manually assigned to the role.

  • Verify Applications After completing the above steps, you will be able to search and access the relevant process order applications in your system.

Reference

For more details, consult the Process Navigator: BJ8, which provides comprehensive guidance on scope item activation and dependencies.

entry.title: SAP Business AI | What | How | Insights
entry.content:
SAP Business AI

SAP Business AI is a comprehensive suite of artificial intelligence technologies and capabilities embedded directly into SAP's enterprise applications and business processes. It represents SAP's strategic approach to delivering relevant, reliable, and responsible AI that transforms how businesses operate by providing intelligent automation, predictive insights, and enhanced decision-making capabilities across the entire enterprise.

Key Components

  • Joule: SAP's AI-powered copilot that provides a unified, conversational interface for interacting with SAP applications using natural language.
  • Embedded AI: AI capabilities natively integrated into core SAP solutions like S/4HANA, SuccessFactors, and Ariba, providing out-of-the-box functionality.
  • AI Foundation: Built on SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP), providing the underlying infrastructure for AI development, deployment, and governance.
  • Joule Agents: Specialized AI agents that handle complex, cross-functional business tasks autonomously or collaboratively.

Primary Capabilities

  • Intelligent Automation: Automates repetitive tasks like invoice processing, document analysis, and workflow management using machine learning and robotic process automation.
  • Predictive Analytics: Leverages machine learning algorithms to forecast trends, predict demand, and enable proactive decision-making across business functions.
  • Natural Language Processing: Enables conversational interactions, document processing, and intelligent content generation through advanced NLP capabilities.
  • Generative AI: Creates new content, summarizes complex information, generates code, and provides creative solutions for business challenges.

Integration with SAP Ecosystem

  • Deep Application Integration: AI capabilities are embedded across SAP's entire product portfolio including S/4HANA, SuccessFactors, Ariba, and SAP Analytics Cloud.
  • Unified Data Foundation: Built on SAP BTP with services like SAP Datasphere and Integration Suite to ensure high-quality data access for AI algorithms.
  • Cross-Application Workflows: Joule provides seamless navigation and task execution across multiple SAP applications through a single interface.
  • Strategic Partnerships: Collaborates with leading AI vendors like Microsoft, Google, AWS, and IBM to leverage cutting-edge technologies and large language models.

Use Cases

  • Finance: Automated invoice processing, cash forecasting, financial reconciliation, and predictive analytics for better financial planning .
  • Supply Chain: Demand forecasting, predictive material requirements planning, and intelligent supply chain optimization.
  • Human Resources: Resume screening, job description generation, personalized learning recommendations, and workforce planning.
  • Procurement: Intelligent spend analysis, contract intelligence, supplier risk detection, and automated sourcing processes .
  • Customer Experience: Personalized recommendations, intelligent customer service, and enhanced user experiences across touchpoints .

Some useful resources

discovering-sap-business-ai

Business AI & Joule

ai-catalog

entry.title: Re: Clarifications on HANA DB access in SAP Private Cloud...
entry.content:
Hi @Nishatharu_Weerakkodi ,

Thank you for the detailed description of your scenario. Let me address each of your points regarding HANA DB access post RISE migration.

1. Direct HANA DB Access in RISE (Private Cloud)
In SAP RISE Private Cloud, direct HANA DB access is significantly restricted compared to On-Premises. SAP manages the underlying infrastructure, and by default, customer created HANA DB users with direct DB access are not carried over or supported in the same way post-migration. SAP's standard position is that direct HANA DB access is limited to specific technical/schema users managed by SAP Basis.

2. Calculation Views & Analysis for Office (AFO)
The good news is that Calculation Views can still be used in S/4HANA 2025. However, the access method changes:
- Direct HANA user login to AFO will likely not be supported in the same way
- SAP recommends using SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) or BW/4HANA as the analytical layer instead
- If direct HANA access is still required, you need to raise a request with SAP to provision specific DB users — this is handled by SAP Basis in RISE and is subject to SAP's approval and security policies
- The 10 custom HANA user IDs and their Calculation View authorizations will not automatically migrate — these need to be re-evaluated and re-provisioned

3. MS Fabric Integration via HANA ODBC
This is the most impacted area. In RISE Private Cloud:
- Direct HANA ODBC connections from external tools like MS Fabric are generally not supported out of the box
- The On-Premises Data Gateway approach will need to be re-architected
- SAP recommends using SAP Datasphere (formerly Data Warehouse Cloud) or OData APIs as the integration layer for external tools
- You will need to work with SAP to evaluate if a read-only technical DB user can be provisioned for this use case, but this is not guaranteed

4. Authorizations Post Migration
Even if user IDs are retained or re-created, authorizations do not automatically carry over. HANA roles and privileges need to be re-assigned in the new system, especially since the schema structure may change between Suite on HANA and S/4HANA 2025.

Recommended Next Steps:
- Raise a formal request with your SAP RISE contract team / Cloud Delivery team to clarify what level of HANA DB access is permitted under your contract
- Engage SAP Early Adopter Care or your SAP Account Executive to discuss the analytical access requirements before migration
- Evaluate SAP Datasphere as a replacement for MS Fabric's direct HANA connection
- Plan for AFO to connect via SAC Live Connection or BW queries rather than direct HANA user login

Hope this helps clarify the impact. I would strongly recommend aligning with SAP on these points early in your migration project to avoid surprises post go-live.

Regards,

Naveen

entry.title: Re: 销售高级首付款申请
entry.content:
Currently there is product limit for region TW adoption the ADP process:

ADP is a new prepayment processing approach introduced by SAP (scope item 7S7, billing type DPR1) that stores prepayment data in a new internal data structure. At this time, no Taiwan enhancements exist for this new flow, and the Taiwan e-invoice XML mapping logic has not yet been adapted to the new structure.

Following KBA: 3754961 - eDocument Taiwan: XML Missing Down Payment Information in Advanced Down Payment Process. You can access it here: https://me.sap.com/notes/3754961.

entry.title: Receive customer payment on behalf of using Advanced Payment Management and In House Banking
entry.content:
Hi All,

My client has a requirement for Receipt on behalf of (ROBO) as below

  1. Company code in UK (Subsidiary 1) invoices a customer in USA.
  2. US Customer makes payment to the bank account of Company code in USA (Susidiary 2).
  3. USA company code posts the bank statement and manually reconcile the customer open item in NL company code via journal.  

We are trying to evaluate if APM with IHB can handle this requirement.

In IHB we have payment scenarios as below which can handle payment via Inhouse bank owner (i.e. corporate group), but our requirement is to receive the payment in US Subsidiary on behalf of NL subsidiary without the involvement of the corporate group.

  1. Central Incoming Payments - Central incoming payments refer to the scenario in which payments for subsidiaries are received via a bank statement of an external bank account belonging to the corporate group that is the owner of the in-house bank
  2. Intercompany Payments - Intercompany payments refer to the scenario in which subsidiary 1 wants to pay an invoice via its in-house bank account to another in-house bank account owned by subsidiary 2

Can we handle this requirement in IHB & APM?

entry.title: Re: the way of cerate index
entry.content:
您好， 是的。可以通过执行SQL进行添加index.
您需要注意几点，

1. 执行SQL的用户需要有权限，例如可能需要SAPHANDB用户来执行。
2. 这个SQL您需要确保语法正确。从SQL上看，index名字有特殊字符，可能语法有问题。
3. 确保加index的column存在于这个表中.

Best regards,
Craig

entry.title: Re: SAP Business Network for Logistics 2605 – What’s New?
entry.content:
Hi, we do monthly releases as to the release schedule. 

You can explore here, what's new on test or productive tenants: https://help.sap.com/docs/business-network-global-track-and-trace#discover_task-whats_new. 

Please note, that we do not publish details on upcoming feature releases BEFORE they become available on test or productive tenants (test usually one month before productive).

entry.title: Re: Microsoft GRAPH API - Read and extract attachments of Outlook email from Cloud Integration
entry.content:
Hi Santosh,

You can connect SAP CPI to cloud Microsoft Outlook / Microsoft 365 using the Mail adapter via IMAP.

Since you are migrating to the cloud, you can replace your inbound Mail/IMAP sender adapter with an integration flow (iFlow) that connects directly to the Microsoft Graph API. If still you wanted to use Microsoft Graph API, even that is also possible.

entry.title: Re: Using WS_DELIVERY_UPDATE_2 in a Loop
entry.content:
That FM is not released for public use. It's unwise to use it if you have any other alternative.

Did you checked BAPI_*_DELIVERY_CHANGE? (* for INB or OUTB)
